name: Kare Ikan (Fish Curry)
recipeInstructions:
Fish curry is the literal translation of kare Ikan. Indonesian 'curry' differs from Indian curries and can be used for almost any fish. Choose a white fish with firm flesh, such as haddock, angler-fish, swordfish or dogfish (whiting in Australia). Americans might use snappers.
1. Heat oil in a heavy frying-pan and carefully brown the fish.
2. In another frying-pan, fry chopped shallots (or onion) and garlic until tender.
3. Stir in chilli, ginger, turmeric, coriander, lemon grass, salam leaf, salt, and tamarind water.
4. Let this mixture simmer for 10 minutes.
5. Add the fish and cover; simmer for another 10 minutes.
6. Add coconut milk and cook for an additional 5 minutes.
7. Serve hot, garnished with thin slices of cucumber and chopped mint. Alternatively, add cucumber and mint during the last 2 minutes of cooking.
Makes 4 servings.
